scenarios.title: 1. Pipeline: tokenize
scenarios.description: pure tokenize cost — `tokenize(source)` for each corpus program

scenarios.title: 2. Pipeline: parse (pre-tokenized)
scenarios.description: parser cost only — `parseTokenStream(pre-tokenized)` for each corpus program

scenarios.title: 3. Pipeline: typecheck (cumulative — incl. tokenize + parse)
scenarios.description: `dvala.typecheck(source)` per program — full pipeline through the typechecker. Typecheck-only cost ≈ this − phase-tokenize − phase-parse.

scenarios.title: 4. Pipeline: run (typecheck disabled)
scenarios.description: `dvala.run(source)` with typecheck disabled — captures tokenize + parse + evaluate. Evaluator-only cost ≈ this − phase-tokenize − phase-parse.

scenarios.title: 5. Pipeline: end-to-end (full)
scenarios.description: `dvala.run(source)` — tokenize + parse + typecheck + evaluate. The number a user actually observes.

scenarios.title: 6. Refinement subtype-check cost (per predicate shape)
scenarios.description: isolated subtype-check calls between source type and refinement target — no parse or typecheck overhead

scenarios.title: 7. Stacked refinement simplify scaling
scenarios.description: simplifying N stacked refinements (`Base & {p1} & {p2} & ... & {pN}`) — empirically O(N²); regressions show as a worse exponent

scenarios.title: 8. Many-inequality refinement worst case
scenarios.description: `Number & {n | n != 1 && n != 2 && ... && n != N}` — documented quadratic worst case (each conjunction step merges against the growing exclusion list)
